rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The amended default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on March 19, 2012 and the amended default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on October 21, 2014 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LLOYD ET AL VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 5. Edward Slaughter, Bill Fountain and Alex Taheri's motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant Y.Y.K. Enterprises, Inc. is granted. Counsel demonstrated they properly served defendant with the motion. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel informs defendant of the tentative ruling and it states that it does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Moving counsel must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: PEDRO RODRIGUEZ ET AL VS. FLOWSERVE US INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 6. Defendant Sellers and Sellers Company's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication: hearing required. At the hearing, the parties shall be prepared to discuss the following: plaintiffs' request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c(h); preference status, its applicability to all defendants, and whether plaintiffs wish to vacate preference status; and the trial date. The Court provides notice that if it grants plaintiffs' continuance request, it will continue the trial date as it relates to Sellers and Sellers Company, for good cause found pursuant to CRC 3.1332 and C.C.P. § 36(f). The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing regarding defendant's motion for summary judgment/adjudication. Additionally, plaintiffs must bring a proposed order to the hearing that grants a trial date continuance. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MILTON JACKSON VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 18, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 7. Brayton Purcell LLP,'s, Nancy Williams' motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiffs Milton Jackson and Marva Jackson is granted. Brayton Purcell demonstrated they properly served plaintiffs. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell informs plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and they state that they do not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)
